docs.title: Basement LA Theme Docs
docs.text:
Theme Docs / Basement LA

Site Status: Live Theme: JUST BASEMENT V.1 Last Reviewed: June 21, 2026

For the team Merchant Guide Step-by-step walkthroughs for every editable part of the theme — header, homepage, product & collection pages, cart, popups, footer, and the standalone pages. View guide →

For developers Developer Docs Architecture plus a per-section reference covering schema settings, metafield dependencies, snippets rendered, and where each section is used. View docs →

Keeping this up to date

This site is static and lives in the same GitHub repo as its own source. When a feature changes in the theme, update the relevant page here as part of that PR — not as a separate "docs day."

docs.title: Make changes with confidence.
docs.text: No code, no guesswork — pick a guide below.

docs.title: Developer Docs
docs.text:
Technical reference for the Basement LA Shopify theme. This theme is built from scratch — no Dawn or other base theme — using Liquid, CSS, and vanilla JS.

This section assumes familiarity with Shopify theme development. It documents how things are built and why, not how to use the admin (see the Merchant Guide for that).

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
Page Covers Theme Architecture Folder structure, section/snippet conventions, schema patterns Sections (nav group) One page per section — schema settings, metafield dependencies, snippets rendered, and where each is used Metafields & Metaobjects (add) Namespace/key reference, Storefront access notes. Note: the current theme uses product metafields only (custom namespace); no metaobjects are referenced — see individual section pages JS Modules (add) How JS is organized, what's modularized vs not Build & Deploy (add) Shopify CLI workflow, theme environments, deploy process

The Sections group in the left nav has a page for every section that ships a {% schema %} block. Start points worth knowing:

  • Collection — also powers the homepage grid; has the in-grid product-banner block.
  • Product — the heaviest metafield consumer (color_label, color_value, size_chart).
  • Cart Sidebar — global, re-renders via the Section Rendering API.
  • Flagged as legacy/unassigned: Homepage, Policy Page.

Source of truth

Code is the source of truth — this page explains intent and reasoning, but if it ever conflicts with the actual codebase, the codebase wins. Update this page in the same PR that changes the relevant code.

docs.title: Refactor / audit tracking
docs.text: Findings from codebase audits (severity-ranked technical debt, dead code, etc.) should live here once generated, so the reasoning behind structural changes is documented alongside the architecture itself.

docs.title: 404 Page
docs.text: sections/404.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Renders the storefront's "Page Not Found" screen — a title and a single call-to-action button pointing shoppers back to a collection. It's a leaf section with no blocks and no dynamic data.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/404.json, which uses the theme layout (so it gets the standard header group, cart/radio sidebars, code-popup, and footer). Preset category: Pages.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default title-text Title Text text Page Not Found shop-btn-text Shop Button Text text Shop All shop-btn-text-color Shop Button Text Color color rgba(255,255,255,1) shop-btn-background-color Shop Button Background Color color #ED1F24 shop-btn-link Shop Button Link url /collections/all

(type: header divider "Shop Button" omitted — it's an editor label only.)

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Purely presentational; all five values are merchant-editable, so don't hardcode the title or button copy in the Liquid.
  • See Theme Architecture for how templates/404.json selects the theme layout.

docs.title: About
docs.text: sections/about-page.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The About page shell. The section itself holds no settings — all content is composed from about-page-* theme blocks, so the entire page is built block-by-block in the editor rather than from section fields.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/page.About.json, using the page-text-theme layout. Preset name: About.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
None. The schema declares "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }], accepting any theme block. In practice the About template wires up these blocks:

  • about-page-section — a layout container
  • about-page-text — rich text content
  • about-page-logo — logo mark
  • about-page-pic-container / about-page-pic — image groupings
  • about-page-link-container / about-page-link — link rows

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly (block templates handle their own markup).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Because there are no section settings, "editing the About page" means editing its blocks — keep that in mind when documenting it for merchants.
  • Block files live in the theme's blocks/ directory; this page only documents the section that hosts them.

docs.title: Announcement Bar
docs.text: sections/announcement-bar.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The thin promo strip at the very top of the site (e.g. "FREE SHIPPING ON US ORDERS 175+"). Supports an optional countdown timer that counts down to a configured drop date/time, with its own color treatment.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text:
Lives in both header section groups — sections/header-group.json and sections/header-group-no-background.json — so it appears on every page whose layout pulls in a header group (i.e. everything except the password page). Preset category: Header Group.

Because it's part of a section group, its saved values differ per group (the two groups ship different announcement copy and drop dates).

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default announcement_text Announcement Text text FREE SHIPPING ON US ORDERS 175+ announcement_text_color Announcement Text Color color #ffffff announcement_background_color Announcement Background Color color #3b3b3bff is-countdown-enabled Is Countdown Enabled? checkbox false date-time-before-drop Date and Time Before Drop text 08/25/2025 15:50:00 time_text_color Time Block Text Color color #3A3A3A time_background_color Time Block Background Color color #d9d9d9ff

date-time-before-drop is a free-text field parsed as mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss — there is no date picker, so the format must be exact or the countdown JS will fail.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The countdown is driven by inline JS reading date-time-before-drop; mistyped dates are a common cause of a stuck or blank timer.
  • Height is part of the layout math — the announcement bar height feeds --sm--announcement-bar-height in snippets/css-variables.liquid. Changing the bar's size means updating that token, not just this section.

docs.title: Folder structure
docs.text:
theme/
├── assets/          # CSS, JS, images, fonts
├── config/          # settings_schema.json, settings_data.json
├── layout/          # theme.liquid and any alternate layouts
├── locales/         # translation files
├── sections/        # reusable, schema-driven page sections
├── snippets/         # render-only partials, no schema
├── templates/        # JSON templates mapping sections to pages
└── blocks/           # (OS 2.0) reusable blocks within sections

docs.title: Conventions used in this theme
docs.text:
• {% render %} over {% include %} — render is scoped (no variable leakage between snippet and parent), which is why it's the default here. include is only used where intentional scope-sharing is needed — flag any new include usage in review.
  • Section schema — (document your naming/setting conventions here once decided)
  • theme.liquid — kept minimal; logic that doesn't need to run on every page should not live here.

docs.title: Metafields & Metaobjects
docs.text:
This theme uses Shopify metaobjects as a managed, reusable value pool for attributes like fit, material, and color — referenced from product metafields rather than hardcoded as plain text. This lets merchants add new values from Shopify admin without a developer touching schema.

Key things to know:

  • Metaobject-referenced metafields require the double .value dereference in Liquid (product.metafields.custom.fit.value.value style access, depending on field type) — easy to miss.
  • Storefront access must be explicitly enabled per metafield definition in Shopify admin, or the reference will not resolve on the storefront even though it shows correctly in admin.
  • Namespace/key paths shown in code examples (e.g. custom.fit_type) are placeholders — always confirm the actual namespace/key in Settings → Custom data before copying a snippet.

docs.title: Performance notes
docs.text: (Document image handling, lazy-loading conventions, section rendering API usage, JS bundling approach, etc. as they're finalized.)

docs.title: Archive
docs.text: sections/archive.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The "Archive" cover page — a horizontally/vertically scrolling gallery of past drops built from archive-item and archive-small-group theme blocks. The section itself only exposes background and scrollbar colors; the items themselves are blocks.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/page.Archive.json, using the page-cover-theme layout (no-background header group + footer, no code-popup is rendered into main). The template instantiates the archive section multiple times with many archive-item and archive-small-group blocks. Preset name: Archive.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default background-color Background Color color rgba(17,17,17,1) scrollbar-foreground-color Scrollbar Foreground Color color #d9d9d9

Content blocks accepted: "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }].

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This is a dark section by default (#111), distinct from the light primary theme background — keep that in mind if restyling.
  • Adding/removing archive entries is a block operation, not a settings change.

docs.title: Buy Together Products
docs.text: sections/buy-together-products-section.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Renders the "Buy Any and Get Additional X% Off" cross-sell module on the product page — a row of products that, when purchased together, trigger a discount code.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/product.json (the theme layout). Preset category: Product.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
The section's own schema contains only an editor note (type: header → "Go to Theme Settings on Side Tab"). All real configuration lives in the global theme settings under Buy Together Products (see Theme Architecture and config/settings_schema.json):

global id label type default buy-together-title Buy Together Title text Buy Any and Get Additional 10% Off buy-together-no-products-text No Products Text text No Products Found buy-together-background-color Background Color color rgba(243,243,243,1) buy-together-collection Buy Together Collection collection — buy-together-max-items-amount Max Product Items number 3 (-1 = unlimited) buy-together-discount-code Discount Code text BUYANYGET10OFF buy-together-discount-amount Discount Amount (%) number 10 mobile--buy-together-product-title-trademark-margin-left Mobile ® margin number 2 desktop--buy-together-product-title-trademark-margin-left Desktop ® margin number 4

Keep code + amount in sync with the actual Shopify discount

buy-together-discount-code must match the discount's Title and buy-together-discount-amount must match its percentage in Shopify Discounts, or the cart total won't reflect what the UI promises.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text:
Reads product metafields (all custom namespace — no metaobjects):

  • custom.buy_together_and_get_featured_products — the featured products list that populates this section.
  • custom.color_label / custom.color_value — color swatch label + hex for each rendered product item.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text:
• processed-product-title — product name with the styled ® trademark.
  • processed-product-title-pure — plain (markup-free) product name.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This is the most "configuration lives elsewhere" section in the theme: editing the section in the editor does almost nothing; the merchant works in Theme Settings.
  • The discount is enforced by Shopify's discount engine, not by this section — the theme only displays it and applies the code.

docs.title: Cart Sidebar
docs.text: sections/cart-sidebar.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The slide-out cart. Shows line items, a free-shipping progress bar, cart recommendations, discount badges, and the checkout button. It re-renders itself via the Section Rendering API: cart actions fetch /?section_id=cart-sidebar and swap #cart-sidebar-container's innerHTML, after which addCartEventListeners() must be re-run (logic in assets/cart.js).

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Rendered globally by layouts, not assigned via a template — it appears in theme, homepage-theme, page-cover-theme, page-text-theme, page-text-theme-no-code-popup, and the GemPages layouts. Preset category: Header Menu.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default show-cart-sidebar Show Cart Sidebar checkbox false show-products-view Show Cart with Products View checkbox false product-show-products-view Product for Show Products View product — mobile--product-title-trademark-margin-left Mobile ® margin number 2 desktop--product-title-trademark-margin-left Desktop ® margin number 4 cart-recommendations-collection Recommendation Collection collection — max-cart-recommendations-amount Max Recommendation Amount number 3 (-1 = unlimited) recommendations-title-text Recommendations Title Text text Recommendations no-products-text No Recommendations Available Text text No Recommendations Available progress-bar-fill-color Free Shipping Bar Fill Color color rgba(17,17,17,1) progress-bar-background-color Free Shipping Bar Background Color color rgba(217,217,217,1) progress-bar-info-text-less Bar Less-Than Text text Away From Free Shipping progress-bar-info-text-achieved Bar Achieved Text text You Get Free Shipping! progress-bar-info-text-not-work No Free Shipping (discounts) Text text Free Shipping does not apply with discounts remove-btn-hover-color Remove Button Hover Color color rgba(207,58,50,1) checkout-extra-text Checkout Extra Text text Taxes and shipping calculated at checkout discount-badge-text-color Discount Badge Text Color color rgba(17,17,17,1) discount-badge-background-color Discount Badge Background Color color (alpha) rgba(84,62,62,0.75) discount-badge-background-blur Discount Badge Background Blur (px) number 5 discount-badge-border-color Discount Badge Border Color color (alpha) rgba(170,170,170,1) discount-badge-border-thickness Discount Badge Border Thickness (px) number 1 empty-cart-text Empty Cart Text text your cart is currently empty... best-sellers-btn-link Best Sellers Button Link url — best-sellers-btn-text-color Best Sellers Button Text Color color rgba(255,255,255,1) best-sellers-btn-background-color Best Sellers Button Background Color color rgba(237,31,36,1) best-sellers-btn-hover-background-color Best Sellers Button Hover Background Color color rgba(212,14,19,1) shop-now-btn-link Shop Now Button Link url —

The free-shipping threshold itself is not here — it's the global free-shipping-amount-threshold setting (in cents-style integer form, e.g. 17500).

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: Reads custom.color_label and custom.color_value (color swatch label + hex) on cart line items and recommendations. No metaobjects.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text:
• processed-product-title
  • processed-product-title-pure

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• show-cart-sidebar and show-products-view are editor-preview toggles — marked "[Leave Disabled in Production!]". They force the cart open so it can be styled in the editor; leaving them on ships an always-open cart.
  • After any innerHTML swap, event listeners are gone — always re-run addCartEventListeners(). This is the single biggest gotcha when modifying cart JS.
  • Keep free-shipping-amount-threshold consistent with the actual free-shipping discount in Shopify.

docs.title: Code Popup
docs.text: sections/code-popup.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A multi-step discount-code capture modal (email → phone → code → claim). Each step is a code-popup-*-view theme block. Visibility is CSS-driven via .display-none on #code-popup-container; the popup appears after a configurable delay.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Rendered globally by layouts — present in theme, page-cover-theme, and page-text-theme. It is commented out in homepage-theme and absent from page-text-theme-no-code-popup (the variant chosen specifically to suppress it, used by Signup and restock-alert). Preset name: Code Popup.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default is-enabled Is Enabled? checkbox true popup-appear-delay Popup Appear Delay (S) number 5 cancel-button-text Cancel Button Text text I don't want my gift cancel-button-text-color Cancel Button Text Color color #060607 cancel-button-background-color Cancel Button Background Color color #595344 cancel-button-border-color Cancel Button Border Color color #060607

The popup's primary and text colors come from the global Code Popup Settings theme settings (code-popup-primary-color, code-popup-text-color), not this section.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly (the step views are blocks).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Two independent ways to turn it off: the is-enabled setting, and choosing a layout that doesn't render it (page-text-theme-no-code-popup). Both exist on purpose — the layout choice guarantees it never fires on conversion-sensitive pages.
  • Steps live in blocks/code-popup-email-view.liquid, …-phone-view, …-code-view, …-claim-view.

docs.title: Collection
docs.text: sections/collection.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The product grid. Handles the filter bar, responsive column counts, "new" and "sold out" badges, and inline promotional banners. Notably, this same section also builds the homepage grid (templates/index.json), not just collection pages.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/collection.json and templates/index.json, both using the theme layout. Preset name: Collection.

docs.title: Section settings
docs.text: id label type default show-product-item-full-title Show Full Product Title checkbox true mobile--min-product-width Mobile Min Product Item Width (PX) number 165 desktop--min-product-width Desktop Min Product Item Width (PX) number 297 desktop--grid-columns Default Desktop Grid Columns range 2–10 4 filter-bar-collection-default Filter Bar Collection Default collection — filter-bar-collection-title-remove-character Title Remove Character text * filter-item-selected-background-color Filter Item Selected Background Color color rgba(217,217,217,1) show-restock-alert-tab Show Restock Alert tab checkbox true restock-alert-tab-label Restock Alert tab label text GET UPDATES restock-alert-page Restock Alert page page — mobile--product-title-trademark-margin-left Mobile ® margin number 1 desktop--product-title-trademark-margin-left Desktop ® margin number 1 product-item-sold-out-overlay-color Sold Out Overlay Color color (alpha) rgba(217,217,217,0.7) no-products-text No Products Found Text text No Products Available product-item-new-badge-text-color New Badge Text Color color rgba(17,17,17,1) product-item-new-badge-background-color New Badge Background Color color rgba(247,231,76,1) product-item-sold-out-badge-text-color Sold Out Badge Text Color color rgba(217,217,217,1) product-item-sold-out-badge-background-color Sold Out Badge Background Color color rgba(86,87,87,1)

docs.title: Block: collection-grid-columns
docs.text:
Overrides the default desktop column count per collection.

id label type default collection-type Collection collection — grid-columns Desktop Grid Columns range 2–10 4

docs.title: Block: product-banner
docs.text:
An in-grid promo banner inserted after the Nth product. Supports same-across-devices or separate mobile/desktop images, each with focal point (_x/_y, 0–100%), zoom (100–200%), width-in-columns (-1 = full row), and pixel height. Plus title, subtitle, text color, and title/subtitle sizes. The mobile/desktop fields are gated by visible_if on image_preference. Rendered through the product-banner snippet.

Key fields: collection-type, insert_after_product (10000 = end), visibility_preference (mobile/desktop/both), image_preference (same/different), image / mobile_image / desktop_image, and the *_width/_height/_x/_y/_zoom groups. See inventory.md for the full field list.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: Reads product.metafields.custom.fitting (product fit label) on grid items. No metaobjects.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text:
• product-banner — renders the in-grid banner block.
  • processed-product-title — product names with the styled ® trademark.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Column resolution order: a matching collection-grid-columns block wins; otherwise the section's desktop--grid-columns is the fallback (1024px and up).
  • filter-bar-collection-title-remove-character (default *) hides a collection from the filter bar when its title contains that character — a content-side toggle.
  • Because this section doubles as the homepage grid, test both templates when changing grid logic.

docs.title: Community Side Panel
docs.text: sections/community-side-panel.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A "Styled By The Community" horizontal scroller on the product page, populated by community-item theme blocks (UGC / lookbook imagery). Includes arrow navigation with a configurable scroll distance.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/product.json (the theme layout), with many community-item blocks. Preset category: Product.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default title Title text Styled By The Community no-items-text No Items Text text No Items Available scroll-amount Arrow Nav Button Scroll Amount (PX) number 600

Content blocks accepted: "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }].

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Individual community tiles are blocks, not settings — adding/removing items is a block operation.
  • scroll-amount is the per-click distance for the arrow nav, mirroring the same setting on related-products.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: sections/contact.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The Contact page form — title, rich-text intro, and the name/email/subject/message inputs with placeholder and error copy, plus submit text. All field labels and helper text are merchant-editable.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/page.Contact.json, using the page-cover-theme layout. The template attaches a single featured-cover-image block (max_blocks: 1). Preset name: Contact.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default title-text Title Text text Get in Touch body-text Body Text richtext <p>Please fill in the form below or email us at <strong>getbasement@gmail.com.</strong></p> name-input-placeholder-text Name Input Placeholder text Name subject-input-placeholder-text Subject Input Placeholder text SUBJECT (ORDER NUMBER IF APPLICABLE) message-input-placeholder-text Message Input Placeholder text Message email-input-placeholder-text Email Input Placeholder text Email email-input-error-text Email Input Error Text text Enter a valid email! submit-text Submit Text text All fields are required to submit. submit-btn-text Submit Button Text text Submit

Accepts one block: "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }], "max_blocks": 1.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• body-text is richtext, so it stores HTML — the default includes a <strong> email address. Editing it through the editor preserves formatting.
  • The contact email shown to shoppers is content, not a setting — it's embedded in body-text.

docs.title: Default Page
docs.text: sections/default-page.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The generic/fallback page section — what a plain Shopify page renders with when no specialized template (About, FAQ, Contact, etc.) applies. It has no settings and no blocks; it simply outputs the page's body content within the text layout.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/page.json (the default page template), using the page-text-theme layout. Preset category: Main.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: None ("settings": [], "blocks": []).

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Nothing here is merchant-editable in the theme editor — page copy is edited under Shopify Admin → Content → Pages, not the customizer. There is intentionally no merchant-guide page for this section.

docs.title: FAQ Page
docs.text: sections/faq-page.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The FAQ page — a title plus a list of expandable question/answer rows. The Q&A rows are faq-dropdown-item theme blocks; the section only owns the heading.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/page.FAQ.json, using the page-text-theme layout. The template wires several faq-dropdown-item blocks (and an ai_gen_block_*). Preset name: FAQ Page.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default title-text Title Text text FAQ

Content blocks accepted: "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }].

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Adding or editing questions is a block operation (faq-dropdown-item), not a settings change — the only section setting is the page title.

docs.title: Footer
docs.text: sections/footer.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The site footer — two menus (main + social), an optional "Early Access" email capture, the Basement logo link, and copyright text, with full color control.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Rendered globally by layouts (page-cover-theme, page-text-theme, page-text-theme-no-code-popup, theme, and theme.gempages.footer) and also referenced explicitly in templates/password.json. Preset category: Layout.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: id label type default footer-main-menu Footer Main Menu link_list — footer-social-menu Footer Social Menu link_list — text-color Text Color color rgba(255,255,255,1) text-hover-color Text Hover Color color rgba(170,170,170,1) background-color Background Color color rgba(17,17,17,1) border-color Border Color color (alpha) rgba(255,255,255,0.1) is-early-access-enabled Enable Early Access? checkbox true label-text Early Access Label Text text Get Early Access placeholder-input-text Early Access Placeholder Input Text text Email basement-logo-link Basement Logo Link url / copyright-text Copyright Text text @Basement 2025.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• footer-main-menu and footer-social-menu are link_list settings — the actual links are managed under Shopify Admin → Content → Menus and selected here.
  • copyright-text is plain text, so the year does not auto-update — it's a manual edit each year.

docs.title: Header Menu
docs.text: sections/header-menu.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The main navigation bar and its associated drawers/sidebars. It composes a set of theme blocks: header-sidebar (mobile menu), header-sidebar-home-view, header-sidebar-shop-view, header-shop-item (the shop tiles), and desktop-header-shop-drawer. It also renders the desktop currency selector.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Lives in both header section groups (header-group.json, header-group-no-background.json), so it's on every page whose layout includes a header group. The two groups differ mainly in does-header-have-background and text colors. Preset category: Header Group.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default does-header-have-background Does Header Have Background? checkbox true text-color Text Color color #111111 text-hover-color Text Hover Color color (alpha) #111111 active-shop-drawer-inactive-text-color Inactive Text Color when Shop Drawer Active color (alpha) rgba(17,17,17,0.5) desktop--shop-menu-item-padding Desktop Shop Menu Item Padding number 8 restock-alert-page Restock Alert page page — restock-alert-menu-label Restock Alert menu label text GET UPDATES

Menu links are not here — they're global theme settings (Header Menu and Header Sidebar groups: header-left-menu, header-right-menu, header-sidebar-main-menu, header-sidebar-soon-menu, plus the menu-item title and header background-color settings).

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: • desktop-currency-selector — the localization (country/currency) selector form.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Header height feeds --sm--header-menu-height in snippets/css-variables.liquid; layout math elsewhere depends on it.
  • does-header-have-background is the key difference between the two header groups — the no-background variant is used by the homepage and cover pages so the hero shows through.
  • The shop tiles in the drawer (header-shop-item blocks) each carry their own image and link; they're configured per block, not in section settings.

docs.title: Homepage
docs.text: sections/homepage.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
A full-screen hero section supporting either a background image or video (separate mobile/desktop sources), an overlay + gradient treatment, and a single "Enter Basement" call-to-action button.

Currently unassigned

This section has a preset ("Homepage") but is not assigned to any template. templates/index.json builds the live homepage from the collection section instead. Treat homepage as a legacy/alternate hero until confirmed with the team — verify before documenting it as the active homepage for merchants.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Not referenced by any template or layout at time of writing. Preset name: Homepage.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default media-type Media Type select (image/video) image mobile--background-image Mobile Background Image image_picker — desktop--background-image Desktop Background Image image_picker — mobile--background-image-x-pos Mobile BG Image X Pos (%) number 0 mobile--background-image-y-pos Mobile BG Image Y Pos (%) number 0 mobile--background-image-zoom Mobile BG Image Zoom (%) number 100 desktop--background-image-x-pos Desktop BG Image X Pos (%) number 0 desktop--background-image-y-pos Desktop BG Image Y Pos (%) number 2 desktop--background-image-zoom Desktop BG Image Zoom (%) number 105 mobile--background-video Mobile Background Video video — desktop--background-video Desktop Background Video video — background-overlay-color Background Overlay Color color (alpha) rgba(0,0,0,0.2) background-graident-color Desktop Background Gradient Color color #000000 desktop--background-graident-opacity Desktop Gradient Opacity (%) range 0–100 30 desktop--background-graident-y-pos Desktop Gradient End Y Pos (%) range 0–100 30 text-color Text Color color #ffffff text Text text Enter Basement btn-link Button Link url /collections/all

Image/video fields are gated by visible_if on media-type.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• "graident" is a misspelling baked into the setting ids (background-graident-color, desktop--background-graident-*) — match it exactly in Liquid; don't "fix" the id or saved values break.
  • Uses Shopify-hosted video settings, so background video is uploaded through the asset picker rather than embedded.

docs.title: Join Waitlist Modal
docs.text: sections/join-waitlist-modal.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A "Notify Me When Available" modal backed by a Klaviyo form. It adapts its layout for product pages vs. other pages via a toggle, and exposes title, background, and backdrop styling.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/product.json, templates/index.json, and templates/collection.json (all theme layout). Preset category: Pages.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: id label type default is-modal-for-product-page Is Modal for Product Page? checkbox false klaviyo-form-id Klaviyo Form ID text — title-text Modal Title Text text Notify Me When Available background-color Background Color color rgba(170,170,170,1) backdrop-color Backdrop Color color (alpha) rgba(17,17,17,0.2)

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• klaviyo-form-id is the random-character ID from the Klaviyo form editor — the modal is inert until it's set.
  • Because the same section is placed on product, index, and collection templates, each placement keeps its own settings — is-modal-for-product-page should be on only for the product-page instance.

docs.title: Password
docs.text: sections/password.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The storefront password page shown when the store is locked (e.g. before a drop). It has a title, a countdown to the drop time, a scrolling image carousel (built from password-item blocks), a password input, and a Klaviyo subscribe form.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/password.json, using the password-theme layout — the only layout with no header group, cart, radio, code-popup, or footer. Preset name: Password.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default show-password-page Show Password Page checkbox false klaviyo-form-id Klaviyo Form ID text — title-text Title Text text New Drop date-time-before-drop Date and Time Before Drop text 08/25/2025 15:50:00 countdown-done-text Countdown Done Text text Please Wait A Moment is-scrolling-img-carousel Scroll Image Carousel? checkbox true img-carousel-scroll-speed Img Carousel Speed number 1 password-input-placeholder-text Password Input Placeholder text Password password-btn-text Password Button Text text Password enter-btn-text Enter Button Text text Enter subscribe-btn-text Subscribe Button Text text Subscribe

Accepts "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }, { "type": "@app" }] — carousel images are password-item blocks.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• show-password-page is an editor-preview toggle ("[Leave Disabled in Production!]") — it lets you style the page in the customizer without actually locking the store. The real lock is Shopify's Online Store → Preferences → Password setting.
  • img-carousel-scroll-speed must be an integer — the inline note warns Safari doesn't handle decimal speeds.
  • date-time-before-drop is free text parsed as mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss.

docs.title: Policy Page Section
docs.text: sections/policy-page-section.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A content container for long-form legal/policy pages (e.g. Privacy Policy). It holds no settings of its own; the page is assembled from policy-page-* theme blocks — headers, text, nested bullet lists, and tables.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned (multiple times) in templates/page.Privacy-Policy.json, using the page-text-theme layout. Preset category: Main.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
None ("settings": []). Content blocks accepted: "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }]. Blocks used by the Privacy Policy template:

  • policy-page-header — section heading
  • policy-page-text — paragraph text
  • policy-page-bullet-parent / policy-page-bullet-item — nested bullet lists
  • policy-page-table / policy-page-table-row / policy-page-table-header / policy-page-table-data — tabular content

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This is the active policy section. The older policy-page section is not referenced by any template and appears superseded.
  • Editing policy content is entirely a block operation — no merchant-guide page is provided since there are no section settings to document.

docs.title: Policy Page
docs.text: sections/policy-page.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
An earlier policy-page container. Like policy-page-section it has no settings and renders policy-page-* theme blocks.

Likely superseded / unused

This section is not referenced by any template. The Privacy Policy template uses policy-page-section instead. Confirm with the team before reusing or removing this section.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Not referenced by any template or layout. Preset category: Main.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: None ("settings": []). Accepts "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }].

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Kept in the inventory for completeness; flagged as a dead-code candidate for the refactor/audit tracking in the Developer Docs overview.

docs.title: Product
docs.text: sections/product.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The product detail page — image gallery (with optional desktop fullscreen view), title with the styled ® trademark, color swatches, size selection, price, preorder handling, and the Sizing / Terms info tabs. This is the largest section in the theme and the heaviest consumer of product metafields.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/product.json, using the theme layout. Uses both @theme and @app blocks (e.g. slogan-container, buy-together-products-block). Preset name: Product.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default show-fullscreen-img Show Desktop Fullscreen Image View? checkbox false nav-btn-color Navigation Button Color color (alpha) rgba(17,17,17,0.5) nav-btn-hover-color Navigation Button Hover Color color (alpha) rgba(17,17,17,0.65) mobile--product-title-trademark-margin-left Mobile ® margin number 2 desktop--product-title-trademark-margin-left Desktop ® margin number 4 thumbnail-btn-selected-border-thickness Thumbnail Btn Selected Border Thickness (px) number 1 preorder-text Preorder Text text Preorder now. Ships in 4-8 weeks. preorder-tag-title Preorder Tag Title text Preorder no-details-text No Details Text text No Details Available no-sizing-text No Size Chart Text text No Size Chart Available no-terms-text No Terms Text text No Terms Available

Many product visuals (size buttons, color items, price slash, etc.) are styled via the global Product Color Settings and Product theme settings, including product-title-trademark-font and product-preorder-text-format.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text:
Reads product metafields (all custom namespace — no metaobjects):

  • custom.color_label / custom.color_value — color swatch label + hex.
  • custom.size_chart — an image rendered in the Sizing tab (accessed via | image_url).

The Terms tab content is currently hardcoded in the section markup (a link to basement.la/pages/terms), not a metafield. Preorder state is derived from the product's tags matching preorder-tag-title / the global preorder format setting.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: • processed-product-title — title + styled ® trademark.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• show-fullscreen-img is an editor-preview toggle ("[Leave Disabled in Production!]") — it forces the fullscreen image view open for styling.
  • Per the theme's own notes, product shipping display moved between metafield keys historically (ship-label → ship-date) — confirm the live key in Liquid before touching shipping/slogan logic.
  • Color swatch rendering here mirrors cart-sidebar, related-products, and buy-together — they all read the same color_label/color_value pair, so a change to that data model ripples across all four.

docs.title: Basement Radio Sidebar
docs.text: sections/radio-sidebar.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A site-wide slide-out audio/radio panel. Tracks are radio-sidebar-track theme blocks; the section controls the panel's color scheme, the mobile exit-arrow label, and a fallback image used when a track's artwork fails to load.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Rendered globally by layouts — theme, homepage-theme, page-cover-theme, page-text-theme, and page-text-theme-no-code-popup. Preset name: Basement Radio Sidebar.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default mobile--exit-arrow-text Mobile Exit Arrow Text text Click Arrow to Exit primary-color Primary Color color #3a3a3a secondary-color Secondary Color color #000000 background-color Background Color color #d7d8d7 button-selected-color Button Selected Color color #999999 radio-default-img Radio Default Image image_picker —

Accepts "blocks": [{ "type": "@theme" }] for the track list.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None directly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Tracks (audio + artwork) are blocks, not settings — managing the playlist is a block operation.
  • radio-default-img is the fallback shown only when a track image fails to load.

docs.title: Related Products
docs.text: sections/related-products.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A "You may also like" horizontal carousel on the product page. Each card is rendered by the related-product-item snippet (image, quick-add plus button with size buttons, title, price). Includes a loading state and arrow navigation.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in templates/product.json (the theme layout). Preset category: Product.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: id label type default title Title Text text You may also like no-products-text No Products Text text No Products Found loading-gif-background-color Loading Gif Background Color color (alpha) rgba(17,17,17,0.5) mobile--product-title-trademark-margin-left Mobile ® margin number 2 desktop--product-title-trademark-margin-left Desktop ® margin number 4 scroll-amount Arrow Nav Button Scroll Amount (PX) number 600

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: The product list comes from product.metafields.custom.related_products (list.product_reference, per the theme's metafield conventions). Each rendered card reads custom.color_label / custom.color_value for its color swatch (via related-product-item). No metaobjects.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: • related-product-item — a single product card. It in turn renders processed-product-title and processed-product-title-pure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Curating which products appear is done by editing the product's custom.related_products metafield, not in the theme editor — only the surrounding copy/colors are section settings.
  • Shares the color_label/color_value swatch model with product, cart-sidebar, and buy-together.

docs.title: Signup
docs.text: sections/signup.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A standalone email-capture / "new drop coming soon" landing section backed by a Klaviyo form. Supports an optional hero image and an optional countdown to the drop.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Assigned in two templates — templates/page.Signup.json and templates/page.restock-alert.json — both using the page-text-theme-no-code-popup layout (so the discount code-popup never fires on these conversion pages). Preset name: Signup.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text: id label type default klaviyo-form-id Klaviyo Form ID text X4vx3V title-text Title Text text NEW DROP COMING SOON subtitle-text Subtitle Text text SIGN UP TO BE THE FIRST TO KNOW show-image Show Image checkbox true image Image image_picker — show-countdown Show Countdown checkbox false date-time-before-drop Date and Time Before Drop text 12/31/2025 15:00:00 countdown-done-text Countdown Done Text text DROP IS LIVE

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• One section, two pages: the Signup page and the Restock Alert page both use it, so each template instance keeps its own form ID, copy, and image.
  • The -no-code-popup layout is chosen deliberately here — don't switch these templates to a code-popup layout.
  • date-time-before-drop is free text parsed as mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss.

docs.title: Spacer
docs.text: sections/spacer.liquid

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: A utility section that inserts vertical whitespace. It can apply one uniform spacing value, or separate mobile and desktop values via a toggle.

docs.title: Where it's used
docs.text: Not placed in any template by default — it's a reusable preset ("Spacer") a merchant can drop between sections in the editor.

docs.title: Schema settings
docs.text:
id label type default has-mobile-and-desktop-variants Has Mobile and Desktop Variants? checkbox false spacing Spacing (PX) number 0 mobile--spacing Mobile Spacing (PX) number 0 desktop--spacing Desktop Spacing (PX) number 0

visible_if gates the fields: spacing shows when the variants toggle is off; mobile--spacing / desktop--spacing show when it's on.

docs.title: Metafields & metaobjects
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Snippets rendered
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Pure layout helper — no content, no data dependencies.
  • Use the variants toggle when mobile and desktop need different gaps; otherwise the single spacing value applies everywhere.

docs.title: Merchant Guide
docs.text:
Format: Click-path + short video/GIF No code required

Each guide below follows the same format: what it changes, where to find it in the theme editor, a short GIF or video of the click-path, and anything to watch out for. Guides are grouped the same way as the sidebar, so start with the section heading that matches what you're trying to change.

docs.title: Homepage & Hero
docs.text:
Not published yet

The homepage is currently built from the Collection Pages grid, not a separate hero section — so there's no guide card here yet. See the developer note if you're not sure which one is live on your site.

docs.title: Header & Footer
docs.text:
Header Header / Navigation Header colors, shop drawer tiles, and the restock-alert link. View guide →

Header Announcement Bar The promo strip at the top of every page, plus its countdown timer. View guide →

Footer Footer Footer menus, the early-access signup, logo link, and copyright. View guide →

docs.title: Product Page
docs.text:
Product Product Pages Preorder messaging, fallback text, and shared product-page styling. View guide →

Product You May Also Like The related-products carousel heading and scroll behavior. View guide →

Product Buy Together & Save The cross-sell discount row — title, collection, and discount code. View guide →

Product Community Panel The "Styled By The Community" scroller heading and photos. View guide →

docs.title: Collections
docs.text: Collection Collection Page Grid columns, the filter bar, badges, and in-grid promo banners. View guide →

docs.title: Sidebars & Popups
docs.text:
Cart Cart Sidebar Free-shipping bar, recommendations, badges, and empty-cart copy. View guide →

Radio Basement Radio Colors and fallback image for the slide-out audio panel. View guide →

Popup Code Popup The gift/discount popup — on/off, delay, and cancel-button copy. View guide →

Popup Waitlist Modal The "Notify Me When Available" popup and its Klaviyo form. View guide →

docs.title: Other Pages
docs.text:
Pages Contact Page Heading, intro text, form placeholders, and the contact email. View guide →

Pages FAQ Page The FAQ heading and the expandable question/answer rows. View guide →

Pages Signup / Restock Alert The "new drop coming soon" signup page and its countdown. View guide →

Pages Password The locked-store page — title, countdown, and image carousel. View guide →

Pages Archive Page Background and scrollbar styling for the Archive page. View guide →

Pages 404 Page The "Page Not Found" headline and shop button. View guide →

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
Layout Spacer Add empty vertical space between sections, no code needed. View guide →

Can't find what you're looking for?

If something you want to change isn't documented yet, check the Developer Docs — it's more technical, but the "Theme Architecture" page lists every editable section by name.

docs.title: Editing the 404 (Page Not Found) Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The headline and the "shop" button shoppers see when they hit a broken or missing URL.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template (top bar) to 404 page → click the 404 Page section in the left sidebar.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 In the template dropdown at the top of the editor, choose 404 page.

02 Select the 404 Page section and edit Title Text (default "Page Not Found").

03 Set the Shop Button Text, Link, and the text/background colors.

04 Click Save.

Test it

Visit any made-up URL on the live site (e.g. /this-page-does-not-exist) to see your changes in context.

docs.title: Editing the Announcement Bar
docs.text:
Header

What it changes: The thin promo strip at the very top of every page (e.g. "FREE SHIPPING ON US ORDERS 175+"), including its optional countdown timer.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → in the left sidebar open the Header group → click Announcement Bar.

docs.title: Editing the Text
docs.text:
01 Open the Header group and select Announcement Bar.

02 Edit Announcement Text and adjust the text/background colors.

03 Click Save.

docs.title: Adding a Countdown
docs.text:
01 Open the Header group and select Announcement Bar.

02 Tick Is Countdown Enabled?.

03 Set Date and Time Before Drop using the exact format mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss (e.g. 11/30/2025 23:59:00).

04 Adjust the Time Block text/background colors if needed, then click Save.

Date format matters

The countdown date is a plain text field — it must be typed exactly as mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss. A mistyped date will make the timer blank or stuck.

docs.title: Styling the Archive Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The background and scrollbar color of the Archive page. (Adding or removing the archive images themselves is done by editing the page's blocks.)

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to the Archive page → click the Archive section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the Archive page in the template dropdown and select the Archive section.

02 Set the Background Color and Scrollbar Foreground Color.

03 To change the actual archive items, expand the section and edit its Archive Item / group blocks.

04 Click Save.

docs.title: Setting Up "Buy Together & Save"
docs.text:
Product

What it changes: The "Buy Any and Get Additional X% Off" cross-sell row on product pages — its title, the collection of products it pulls from, and the discount it applies.

Where to find it: This section is controlled almost entirely from Theme Settings, not the section itself. Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → click the gear / Theme settings icon at the bottom of the left sidebar → Buy Together Products.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open Theme settings → Buy Together Products.

02 Set the Buy Together Title and choose the Buy Together Collection (the pool of eligible products).

03 Enter the Discount Code and Discount Amount (%) so they match a real Shopify discount.

04 Click Save.

Match the actual discount

The Discount Code must equal the discount's Title in Shopify Admin → Discounts, and the Discount Amount (%) must equal its percentage. If they don't match, the cart total won't apply the savings the product page promises.

Per-product featured items

Which specific products appear can also be driven by a product's Buy together & get featured products metafield. If a product shows the wrong items, check its metafields — see the developer notes.

docs.title: Editing the Cart (Slide-Out)
docs.text:
Cart

What it changes: The slide-out cart panel — the free-shipping progress bar text and colors, cart recommendations, discount badges, empty-cart message, and the checkout helper text.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → click Cart Sidebar in the left sidebar (it appears on every page).

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Cart Sidebar section. To see it while editing, temporarily tick Show Cart Sidebar.

02 Choose a Recommendation Collection and set the Max Recommendation Amount (use -1 for unlimited).

03 Edit the Free Shipping Bar text and colors, plus the Empty Cart message and buttons.

04 Untick Show Cart Sidebar again, then click Save.

Turn the preview toggles back off

Show Cart Sidebar and Show Cart with Products View are for previewing only. Leave both disabled before saving for production, or shoppers will see the cart forced open.

Free shipping amount lives elsewhere

The dollar amount that unlocks free shipping is set in Theme settings → Free Shipping, and must match your actual free-shipping discount in Shopify.

docs.title: Editing the Discount Code Popup
docs.text:
Popup

What it changes: The gift/discount-code popup that appears after a short delay — whether it's on, how long before it shows, and the cancel-button wording and colors.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → click Code Popup in the left sidebar.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Code Popup section.

02 Use Is Enabled? to turn the popup on or off, and set the Popup Appear Delay (S).

03 Edit the Cancel Button text and colors. (The popup's main accent/text colors are in Theme settings → Code Popup Settings.)

04 Click Save.

It won't show on every page

The popup is intentionally suppressed on the homepage and on the Signup / Restock Alert pages, so it never interrupts those flows.

docs.title: Editing Collection Pages (Product Grid)
docs.text:
Collection

What it changes: The product grid on collection pages (and the homepage grid) — number of columns, the filter bar, "New"/"Sold Out" badges, and in-grid promo banners.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to a Collection page → click the Collection section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Collection section and set Default Desktop Grid Columns (2–10).

02 To override columns for one specific collection, add a Collection Grid Columns block, pick the collection, and set its column count.

03 To place a promo image in the grid, add a Product Banner block: choose the collection, set Insert After Product # (use 10000 for the end), pick the image, and adjust focal point/zoom.

04 Adjust the New Badge and Sold Out colors as needed, then click Save.

This also controls the homepage grid

The same Collection section builds the product grid on the homepage. Changes to grid behavior can affect both — check the homepage after editing.

docs.title: Editing the "Styled By The Community" Panel
docs.text:
Product

What it changes: The "Styled By The Community" scroller on product pages — its heading, the "no items" fallback text, and how far the arrow buttons scroll. (The photos themselves are blocks.)

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to a Product page → click the Community Side Panel section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open a Product template and select the Community Side Panel section.

02 Edit the Title (default "Styled By The Community").

03 To add or change photos, expand the section and edit its Community Item blocks.

04 Click Save.

docs.title: Editing the Contact Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The Contact page heading, intro text, the form field placeholders, and the submit button wording.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to the Contact page → click the Contact section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the Contact page in the template dropdown and select the Contact section.

02 Edit the Title Text and the Body Text (the rich-text intro, including the contact email).

03 Adjust the input placeholders (Name, Email, Subject, Message) and the Submit Button Text.

04 Click Save.

Changing the contact email

The email address shoppers are told to write to lives inside the Body Text field — edit it there.

docs.title: Changing the Homepage Banner
docs.text:
Homepage

What it changes: The large image and headline at the top of the homepage.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → Homepage → click the Hero Banner section in the left sidebar.

docs.title: Quick preview
docs.text:
Drop a short (5-10 second), compressed GIF at docs/assets/gifs/homepage-banner.gif and reference it like this:

Keep GIFs under ~5MB — they're checked into the repo, so size adds up over time. ezgif.com or gifski work well for compressing screen recordings.

docs.title: Full walkthrough (longer video)
docs.text:
For anything that takes more than ~15 seconds to show, upload an unlisted YouTube video (or a Loom link) instead of a GIF, and embed it:

<iframe width="100%" height="400"
  src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID_HERE"
  title="Changing the Homepage Banner"
  frameborder="0" allowfullscreen>
</iframe>

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the theme editor and select the Hero Banner section.

02 Click Image to upload a new banner image (recommended size: 2400×1200px, <500KB).

03 Edit the Heading and Subheading text fields.

04 Click Save in the top right.

Image size

Oversized images here will slow down the homepage. Compress before uploading — see the Developer Docs: Performance notes on image handling if you want the technical reason why.

docs.title: Editing the FAQ Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The FAQ page heading and the expandable question/answer rows.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to the FAQ page → click the FAQ Page section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the FAQ page in the template dropdown and select the FAQ Page section.

02 Edit the Title Text (default "FAQ").

03 To add, edit, or reorder questions, expand the section and edit its FAQ Dropdown Item blocks (each block is one question + answer).

04 Click Save.

docs.title: Editing the Footer
docs.text:
Footer

What it changes: The site footer — its menus, the "Get Early Access" email signup, the logo link, the copyright line, and footer colors.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → click the Footer section in the left sidebar.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Footer section.

02 Choose the Footer Main Menu and Footer Social Menu from your saved menus.

03 Toggle Enable Early Access? and edit its label/placeholder, then update the Copyright Text.

04 Click Save.

Menus are managed separately

The links inside the footer menus are edited under Shopify Admin → Content → Menus. Here you only pick which menu appears.

Copyright year is manual

The copyright text doesn't update automatically — change the year here each January.

docs.title: Editing the Header / Navigation
docs.text:
Header

What it changes: The top navigation bar — its colors, whether it has a background, the shop drawer tiles, and the restock-alert link. (The menu links are set in Theme settings.)

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → open the Header group in the left sidebar → click Header Menu.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the Header group and select Header Menu.

02 Set the Text Color and Does Header Have Background? to suit the page background.

03 Edit the shop tiles (images + links) via the Header Shop Item blocks inside the drawer/sidebar.

04 Click Save.

Menu links live in Theme settings

The actual navigation links (left menu, right menu, sidebar menus) are chosen in Theme settings → Header Menu and Header Sidebar, and the links themselves are built under Content → Menus.

Two header versions

The homepage and cover pages use a "no background" header so the hero shows through. If a change doesn't appear there, edit that page's header group too.

docs.title: Editing the Homepage Hero
docs.text:
Homepage

What it changes: The full-screen hero at the top of the homepage — its background image or video, the overlay/gradient, and the "Enter Basement" button.

Confirm this section is in use first

The current homepage is built from the Collection grid section, and this Homepage hero section may not be active on the live site. Before relying on this guide, confirm with a developer whether the Homepage section is assigned to the homepage template. See the developer note.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → Home page template → click the Homepage section (if present).

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Homepage section and choose Media Type — Image or Video.

02 Upload the Mobile and Desktop background image (or video), then nudge the X/Y position and zoom so the focal point sits right on each device.

03 Adjust the Background Overlay / gradient, then set the Enter Button text, color, and link.

04 Click Save.

Compress large media

Oversized hero images/videos slow the homepage down. Compress images before uploading and keep background video short and light.

docs.title: Editing the "Notify Me" Waitlist Modal
docs.text:
Popup

What it changes: The "Notify Me When Available" popup (waitlist / restock signup) — its title, colors, and the connected Klaviyo form.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → select the template where it appears (Product, Home page, or Collection) → click the Join Waitlist Modal section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Join Waitlist Modal section on the relevant template.

02 Paste your Klaviyo Form ID (the short random code from the Klaviyo form editor).

03 Edit the Modal Title Text and the background/backdrop colors.

04 Click Save.

It needs a Klaviyo Form ID

Without a valid Klaviyo Form ID, the modal won't capture signups. On the product-page copy of this section, leave Is Modal for Product Page? enabled so it uses the product layout.

docs.title: Editing the Password (Coming Soon) Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The password-protected "New Drop" page shown while the store is locked — the title, countdown, image carousel, and the password/subscribe buttons.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template (top bar) to Password → click the Password section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 In the template dropdown choose Password, then select the Password section. Temporarily tick Show Password Page so you can see it while editing.

02 Set the Title Text, the Date and Time Before Drop (mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss), and the Countdown Done Text.

03 Add carousel images via the section's blocks, and paste your Klaviyo Form ID for the subscribe form.

04 Untick Show Password Page, then click Save.

Turning the lock on/off is separate

The Show Password Page toggle here is only for previewing in the editor. The real store lock is controlled in Online Store → Preferences → Password page. Leave Show Password Page disabled before saving.

Carousel speed

Keep Img Carousel Speed a whole number (no decimals) — decimals can break the scroll in Safari.

docs.title: Editing Product Pages
docs.text:
Product

What it changes: Shared settings for every product page — navigation button colors, the preorder message, and the fallback text shown when a product is missing details, a size chart, or terms.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to a Product page → click the Product section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open a Product template and select the Product section.

02 Edit the Preorder Text and Preorder Tag Title — products tagged with that title will show the preorder message.

03 Adjust the "Not Available" fallback texts (No Details / No Size Chart / No Terms) if needed.

04 Click Save.

Per-product content comes from the product itself

Images, description, color swatches, and the size chart are set on each product in Shopify Admin → Products (via product fields and metafields), not in this section. This section only controls the shared layout and fallback wording.

Leave the preview toggle off

Show Desktop Fullscreen Image View? is for editor preview only — keep it disabled for production.

docs.title: Editing the Basement Radio Panel
docs.text:
Radio

What it changes: The slide-out Basement Radio (audio) panel — its colors, the mobile exit-arrow label, and the fallback image. (The tracks themselves are blocks.)

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → click the Basement Radio Sidebar section in the left sidebar.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Select the Basement Radio Sidebar section.

02 Set the Primary, Secondary, Background, and Button Selected colors.

03 Upload a Radio Default Image (shown if a track's artwork fails to load), and edit tracks via the section's blocks.

04 Click Save.

docs.title: Editing "You May Also Like"
docs.text:
Product

What it changes: The "You may also like" carousel on product pages — its heading, the "no products" fallback text, and how far the arrows scroll.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to a Product page → click the Related Products section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open a Product template and select the Related Products section.

02 Edit the Title Text (default "You may also like").

03 Adjust Arrow Nav Button Scroll Amount if the carousel scrolls too far or too little.

04 Click Save.

Which products show is set per product

The actual recommended products come from each product's Related products metafield in Shopify Admin → Products, not from this section. Here you only edit the wording and behavior.

docs.title: Editing the Signup / Restock Alert Page
docs.text:
Pages

What it changes: The "New Drop Coming Soon" signup page (also used for the Restock Alert page) — its heading, subtitle, optional image, optional countdown, and the connected Klaviyo form.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → switch the template to the Signup page (or Restock Alert page) → click the Signup section.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Open the Signup (or Restock Alert) page in the template dropdown and select the Signup section.

02 Paste the Klaviyo Form ID, then edit the Title Text and Subtitle Text.

03 Optionally toggle Show Image / Show Countdown and set the Date and Time Before Drop (mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss).

04 Click Save.

Same section, two pages

The Signup page and the Restock Alert page both use this section, but each keeps its own settings — update the one whose template you're editing.

docs.title: Adding Spacing Between Sections
docs.text:
Layout

What it changes: Adds empty vertical space between sections. Useful for breathing room without touching code.

Where to find it: Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize → on any page, click Add section → choose Spacer.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
01 Click Add section where you want the gap, and choose Spacer.

02 Set Spacing (PX) for a single value across all devices.

03 Need different gaps on mobile vs desktop? Tick Has Mobile and Desktop Variants? and set Mobile Spacing and Desktop Spacing separately.

04 Drag the Spacer into position and click Save.
